MOUNTAIN CITY REHAB CENTER

CCN: 215277 · FROSTBURG, MD 21532 · Allegany County

Overview

Address
48 TARN TERRACE, FROSTBURG, MD 21532
Phone
3016891391
Certified beds
88
Avg daily residents
85 (97% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit LLC
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1995-08-17
Setting
Rural
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.33 3.86 3.33 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.65 0.67 0.65 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.67 0.86 0.67 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.02 2.33 2.02 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.31 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.06
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.45
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.06
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.37
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.99
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-26 46 37 26 288 3 490
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2019-09-20 6 6 0 24 1 24
Total weighted health score
373.5
lower is better

Deficiencies (71)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

4 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
64 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
1 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. E 2025-11-07 2026-01-07
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0578 Honor the resident's right to request, refuse, and/or discontinue treatment, to participate in or refuse to participate in experimental research, and to formulate an advance directive. E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0688 Provide appropriate care for a resident to maintain and/or improve range of motion (ROM), limited ROM and/or mobility, unless a decline is for a medical reason. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0698 Provide safe, appropriate dialysis care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0838 Conduct and document a facility-wide assessment to determine what resources are necessary to care for residents competently during both day-to-day operations (including nights and weekends) and emergencies. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0655 Create and put into place a plan for meeting the resident's most immediate needs within 48 hours of being admitted E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. E 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-11-26 2022-12-06
0726 Ensure that nurses and nurse aides have the appropriate competencies to care for every resident in a way that maximizes each resident's well being. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0776 Provide timely, approved x-ray services, or have an agreement with an approved provider to obtain them. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0851 Electronically submit to CMS complete and accurate direct care staffing information, based on payroll and other verifiable and auditable data. F 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. D 2024-11-26 2024-12-20
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2024-11-26 2025-01-31
0576 Ensure residents have reasonable access to and privacy in their use of communication methods. C 2019-09-20 2019-11-30
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2019-09-20 2019-11-30
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. D 2019-09-20 2019-11-30
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2019-09-20 2019-11-30

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
